page: Vultr voc-c-1c-2gb-25s-amd is an entry-level virtual machine within the Optimized Cloud Compute family. It is equipped with a single shared vCPU powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz and features 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. This instance is configured with 2.0 GB of system memory and includes 25 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. No GPU hardware is included with this server type.

Performance benchmarks reveal a highly polarized capability profile. Due to its single-core architecture, the instance achieves top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, scoring 3373.08, but ranks in the bottom 25% for multi-core workloads. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks show strong performance, with gzip compression reaching 31.6 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.2 GB/sec. However, memory bandwidth is relatively constrained, with read-only small block cached bandwidth at 165.89 GB/sec and large block uncached read bandwidth at 35.29 GB/sec, both falling into the bottom 25% tier.

The primary tradeoffs of this instance are its shared CPU allocation, limited memory capacity, and low database throughput. Passmark database operations score at 1117.66 and Redis SET operations are limited to 2.72 million operations per second, while static web serving throughput is low at 1.65 GB/sec. Qualitatively, this server represents a highly cost-efficient entry point for lightweight, single-threaded tasks, development environments, basic utility scripting, or low-traffic web serving where high resource density and low cost are prioritized over sustained multi-core performance.
description: Vultr voc-c-1c-2gb-25s-amd is an entry-level, shared-CPU instance within the Optimized Cloud Compute family. It is configured with 1 vCPU from an AMD EPYC Genoa processor (x86_64 architecture) running at 2.0 GHz, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 25 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. While its single-core performance is highly competitive—achieving top-tier results in stress-ng single-core tests and strong single-threaded compression speeds—its overall multi-core, memory bandwidth, and database performance are heavily constrained by its single-core, shared-resource design. Passmark database operations and Redis SET benchmarks reflect these limitations, placing the instance in the lower performance tiers. This server is qualitatively a highly cost-efficient option for lightweight workloads, single-threaded utility scripts, microservices, and development environments that do not require sustained multi-core processing or large memory footprints.
og_description: Vultr voc-c-1c-2gb-25s-amd is an entry-level virtual server featuring 1 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPU, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 25 GB of NVMe SSD storage, optimized for lightweight single-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Vultr voc-c-1c-2gb-25s-amd is a shared-CPU instance with 1 AMD EPYC Genoa vCPU, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 25 GB of NVMe storage for lightweight workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly, single-core virtual server with NVMe storage, optimized for lightweight single-threaded tasks and development environments.
bullet_points: 1 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPU (x86_64 architecture)
2.0 GB of system memory and 25 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Excellent single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Constrained memory bandwidth and multi-core processing capabilities
Best suited for lightweight utility scripts, microservices, and development environments
